steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: An x-intercept is a value of $x$ where $f(x) = 0$. The question asks how many distinct such values exist. A double root (where the graph is tangent to the x-axis) counts as one distinct value, not two.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'One': The graph crosses or touches the axis at more than one location. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Two': The graph touches the x-axis at one point (tangent, double root) and crosses it at one other point — two distinct values. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Three': Three distinct zeros would require the graph to cross or touch the axis at three separate x-values, which it does not. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Four': A cubic $f(x) = ax^3 + bx^2 + cx + d$ can have at most three real roots. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
